name: SOC 2
description: SOC 2 Trust Service Criteria — AICPA framework for managing customer data based on five trust service principles: security, availability, processing integrity, confidentiality, and privacy.

families.description: CC6 — Common Criteria for logical and physical access to systems, data, and facilities.

controls.title: Logical and Physical Access Controls
controls.description: The entity implements logical access security software, infrastructure, and architectures over protected information assets to protect them from security events to meet the entity's objectives.

controls.title: Authorization of New Users
controls.description: Prior to issuing system credentials and granting system access, the entity registers and authorizes new internal and external users whose access is administered by the entity.

controls.title: User Authentication
controls.description: The entity requires identification and authentication of users prior to granting access to system resources and data.

controls.title: Access Removal and Modification
controls.description: The entity removes or modifies access of users whose role or employment has changed or has been terminated.

controls.title: Privileged User Access Review
controls.description: The entity grants access to data, system resources, infrastructure, and system configurations based on job responsibilities with the least privilege principle and segregation of duties where applicable.

controls.title: Physical Access Controls
controls.description: The entity implements physical access controls to limit access to hardware and computing infrastructure based on authorized roles.

families.description: CC7 — Common Criteria for system operations including monitoring, incident response, and capacity management.

controls.title: Detection and Monitoring of Security Events
controls.description: To meet its objectives, the entity uses detection and monitoring procedures to identify anomalies, security events, and threats.

controls.title: Response to Security Incidents
controls.description: The entity responds to identified security incidents by executing a defined incident response program to understand, contain, remediate, and communicate security incidents, as appropriate.

controls.title: Evaluation and Management of Changes
controls.description: The entity identifies, develops, and implements activities to manage changes to system components and their related processes.

controls.title: Capacity Management and Performance
controls.description: The entity monitors the capacity and performance of operating systems, databases, and other system components to meet its objectives.

controls.title: System Backup and Recovery
controls.description: The entity maintains backup and recovery procedures to meet its objectives.

families.description: CC8 — Common Criteria for managing changes to systems and infrastructure.

controls.title: Change Management Program
controls.description: The entity authorizes, designs, develops or acquires, configures, documents, tests, approves, and implements changes to infrastructure, data, software, and procedures to meet its objectives.

controls.title: Change Authorization and Approval
controls.description: Changes are authorized, tested, approved, and communicated prior to implementation.

controls.title: Emergency Change Procedures
controls.description: The entity implements emergency change procedures that are documented and approved by management to respond to urgent system issues.

controls.title: Development and Acquisition Standards
controls.description: The entity follows defined system development life cycle standards that address security and availability requirements throughout the development process.
